summary: An outstanding summer‑’90 show with crystal‑clear audience recording, highlighted by a monster China > Rider and a sizzling Estimated freakout.
review: Fans rave about the superb audio – crisp instrument separation that makes this arguably the best audience tape of the era – and the high‑energy performance.  Brent’s vocals and keyboards are on fire, Jerry’s midi‑enhanced guitar shines, and the second set delivers a legendary China > Rider jam plus an unforgettable Estimated freakout.  Minor gripes (a slightly slow tempo and a faster‑than‑ideal Truckin') do little to dent the overall excitement.

ai_rating.rationale: Over a dozen enthusiastic 4–5‑star reviews praise both musical performance and audio fidelity; only minor tempo comments appear, so the combined show (70%) and recording (30%) score is very high.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Described as ‘crispy’, with superb instrument separation; many call it the best audience tape they’ve heard. One reviewer suspects a matrix blend, but overall the sound is clear, balanced and free of noticeable drop‑outs.

poor_songs: Overall tempo felt a bit slow
show_quality.setlist_flow: A high‑energy summer ’90 set with an unusual opener (LTGTR > Truckin' > Touch of Grey) that sets a ‘no‑messing‑around’ vibe, followed by a jam‑heavy second set anchored by the epic China > Rider and extended Estimated freakout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for crisp guitar tone, creative midi effects and overall brilliance; described as “BREEEEENT”.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass work noted on the China jam and a few ‘phil‑bombs’; saves the day later in the set.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bobby’s vocal improvisations on Estimated highlighted as one of his top ten freakouts.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s keyboards and vocals are described as “on fire”, delivering strong counterpoint and memorable moments.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey’s Japanese‑style drums and the dual‑drummer runs on Terrapin are singled out for excitement.
